text:
Market today: Rate hike whispers are the real story today. The Warsh/Fed chatter is injecting fresh uncertainty into growth and tech — exactly the names that fill your book. Higher rates compress valuations on high-multiple stocks hardest, and you're on margin, so that math hits twice.

Your holdings: PLTR is down 13% and flagged SELL — no cushion there, and it's the most rate-sensitive story stock you own. AVGO is your largest position, down 12% and flagged TRIM — size plus loss plus margin is a dangerous combo. PTNM is down 15% with only a 9% buffer to its stop at $9.50 — tiny spec, tiny room. SPCX is brand-new, very volatile IPO with limited data; manage it manually and keep size small. Margin amplifies every one of these drops.

Watch: (1) Any Fed/Warsh follow-up commentary — one hawkish headline could reprice your whole book fast. (2) AVGO price action — largest position down 12%, a further leg down triggers real damage.

Bottom line: Your two biggest fires are PLTR (act on the SELL signal) and AVGO (trim the size before it trims you).
